message:
fix: replace unbounded channels with bounded sync_channels and improve stream receive semantics

Two related improvements to the extension subprocess stream processing
pipeline that reduce memory pressure and CPU waste:

Bounded channels (staged):
- extension_dispatcher.rs: Replace mpsc::channel() with
  mpsc::sync_channel(1024) and mpsc::sync_channel(128) for exec
  subprocess stream frames. Change pump_stream Sender to SyncSender.
  This adds backpressure — when the consumer falls behind, producers
  block instead of unboundedly queueing data in memory.

- extensions.rs: Change hostcall exec_ref pump_stream to accept
  SyncSender, matching the bounded channel change above.

Receive semantics (unstaged):
- extension_dispatcher.rs: Replace thread::sleep(10ms) busy-wait with
  rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(10)) in the process stream
  drain loop. This wakes immediately when data arrives instead of
  always sleeping the full 10ms, reducing latency for bursty output
  while maintaining the same timeout ceiling.

- extensions.rs: Replace rx.recv_timeout(25ms) with rx.try_recv()
  plus explicit extension_wait_sleep(25ms) on Empty. This separates
  the "check for data" and "yield to async runtime" concerns — the
  async sleep cooperates with the extension scheduler rather than
  blocking the thread, improving concurrent extension throughput.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
